artPrompt: A dramatic election-night moment: a single oversized die hangs frozen in mid-air above a feral town square, ringed by goblins leaning in with held breath, flame-orange bonfire light flaring up the wide frame against night-black smoke. Confetti and sparks streak around the suspended cube. A doom-laden prediction caught the instant before the roll, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Exit Poll of Doom
text:
Ask each opponent, one at a time, to silently predict the result of a six-sided die before you roll it. Go around the table.
Then roll the die. Each opponent who guessed wrong loses life equal to how wrong they were (off by 3? lose 3). Each opponent who guessed right looks smug; that's punishment enough, they lose nothing.
The pollster (you) does not guess. The pollster never guesses. The pollster knows.

extras.physicalAction: have opponents guess a die roll
